message:
feat(agent-drivers): add AbortSignal support to event subscription

Extends the subscribe() method to accept an optional AbortSignal,
enabling callers to cancel subscriptions without terminating agents.

Problem:
Event subscriptions had no cancellation mechanism other than agent
termination. This prevented clean shutdown of event handlers when
the consuming code needed to stop listening (e.g., during hot-reload
or reconfiguration).

Solution:
- Add optional `signal?: AbortSignal` parameter to subscribe()
- Wire abort handling to clean up subscription state immediately
- Prevent event leaks by clearing queue on abort
- Add test verifying subscription cleanup without termination

Changes:
- packages/agent-drivers/src/interface.ts: Add signal param to interface
- packages/agent-drivers/src/base-driver.ts: Implement abort handling
  - Register abort listener that sets cancelled flag
  - Clear queue and resolve pending promise on abort
  - Clean up event listener in finally block
- packages/agent-drivers/src/__tests__/base-driver.test.ts: Add abort test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
